symptoms.symptom_name: Syncope triggered immediately by a loud acoustic startle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auditory-startle-triggered syncope is a hallmark of LQT2.
An instantaneous startle trigger without prodrome is atypical for vasovagal syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Convulsive syncope (brief stiffening) with rapid recovery, no tongue-biting/post-ictal state

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Convulsive syncope from transient cerebral hypoperfusion is consistent with an arrhythmic cause.
Rapid recovery without tongue-biting or post-ictal confusion argues against a primary seizure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of young sudden death (maternal aunt at 25)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Young sudden death in a relative markedly raises the probability of an inherited channelopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain or palpitations before the event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a warning is consistent with an abrupt arrhythmic syncope and does not lower concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: No QT-prolonging drugs or regular medications

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of an acquired (drug) cause makes a congenital channelopathy more likely as the explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Structurally normal heart with normal resting examination and pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal structural exam is expected in LQTS (an electrical disorder) and does not exclude it, while arguing against structural causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTc >470 ms) on 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG with QTc measurement (and continuous monitoring)
investigation_details.expected_result: Prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms) confirming Long QT syndrome, with monitoring for Torsades
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Long QT syndrome cannot be confirmed and the risk of fatal Torsades cannot be managed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prolonged QTc on ECG is the diagnostic hallmark of Long QT syndrome.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acoustic-startle-triggered convulsive syncope with a young sudden-death family history and a structurally normal heart strongly indicates Long QT syndrome, demanding immediate ECG/monitoring, trigger avoidance and specialist assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Measures the corrected QT interval - the diagnostic hallmark; must be done urgently in a monitored setting.
Detects Torsades de Pointes/arrhythmia during the high-risk period.
Confirms a structurally normal heart and guides diagnosis, risk stratification and therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance and discharge as a simple faint without ECG
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Clearing this without an ECG risks a fatal missed inherited arrhythmia.
